content.text: Found it: the `es-config` ConfigMap has `node.name:` set to an empty/null value instead of `${POD_NAME}`, which crashes any Elasticsearch process that (re)reads it at startup — that's why `es-cluster-2` can't rejoin after its restart (the other two pods just haven't been restarted since the bad edit).

input.command:
kubectl get cronjob,job -n elasticsearch
echo "=== index settings ==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s "es-http:9200/app-data/_settings?pretty"
echo "=== snapshot repos ==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s "es-http:9200/_snapshot?pretty"
echo "=== check all pvcs across namespaces for stray es data ==="
kubectl get pvc -A
echo "=== check pods for a seed/writer job image ==="
kubectl get pods -A -o wide | grep -i writ
